title: Parrya junussovii
intro: Parrya junussovii, commonly known as the Mongolian parny or wolf's milk, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Brassicaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions including fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Parrya junussovii include glucosinolates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Parrya junussovii is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, as well as being used in the form of dried extract or tincture.
